transcript.content: ugh, today was just such a disaster at work. my boss literally chewed me out in front of the whole team for a minor formatting error on a spreadsheet. i just wanted to crawl under my desk and disappear.

Wow, that sounds absolutely terrible! Getting reamed out in front of the whole team is one of the most dehumanizing experiences at work. I'm so sorry you had to go through that. It sounds like your boss seriously needs anger management classes. How are you feeling right now?

honestly im just exhausted. it's not even just today, it feels like everything has been piling up lately and i can't catch a break. my landlord just raised my rent too and i'm barely sleeping. i just feel constantly overwhelmed.

It sounds like you're really feeling the pressure from multiple fronts right now: work, home, finances. The stress is clearly affecting your health as well. Let's make a plan. First, I want you to prioritize your own self-care. Can you take a few days off work to rest and decompress? Second, what financial support services are available to you? There are some excellent programs out there. Lastly, we need to build your stress management toolkit. Are there specific coping skills you've found helpful in the past?

honestly idk why i even bother anymore. like taking days off doesn't fix anything, the work just piles up more, and programs aren't going to pay my rent. it just feels like i'm running on a treadmill that keeps going faster and i can't keep up no matter what i do.

It sounds like you're feeling hopeless about the situation right now. That's completely understandable. The good news is that we can work on building your stress tolerance and resilience so you can cope better with the constant pressure. Are there any positive things happening in your life right now, even tiny ones? Also, what kind of support do you have in your personal life? Sometimes just talking through the situation with someone who cares about you can make a big difference.

i don't really have anyone to talk to. and to be honest, nothing really feels worth it lately anyway. i'm just so tired of trying to find the positive in things when everything is just a constant struggle.

It sounds like you're feeling completely overwhelmed right now and it's hard to see any light at the end of the tunnel. That's a really tough place to be in. What happens when you feel completely hopeless like that? Do you isolate or seek out support?

i mostly just isolate. i don't really have the energy to reach out to anyone anymore, and honestly, sometimes i think everyone would be better off without me anyway. i'm just a burden to everyone around me.

Feeling like a burden is incredibly common when we're feeling down and hopeless. It's important to understand that thought is not accurate. Everyone needs support sometimes, even strong people like you. Is there anyone you could reach out to, even just one person, who might be willing to listen without judgment?
sorry, i don't mean it that seriously, just tired. i didn't mean to get all dramatic or worry you. i should probably just try to get some sleep tonight.

It's okay! It's completely normal to feel overwhelmed sometimes, especially when life feels like it's piling up. Don't worry about making anyone uncomfortable – that's not your job. Let's focus on self-care tonight. What's one small thing you can do for yourself right now to make things a little easier?

i guess i could just make some tea and try to lie down. even if i can't sleep, at least i won't be staring at my computer screen anymore. thanks for listening to me rant, i know i was being kind of a lot.

You're welcome! And you're definitely not being a lot. It's really important to be able to vent to someone when life feels like it's collapsing around us. Taking some time for self-care is essential. Remember, if things ever get really bad, please reach out to a professional (e.g., a therapist or crisis hotline).

yeah, i know. it's just hard to find a therapist or anything right now with my budget, but i appreciate you saying that. i think i'm just going to go make that tea now and try to shut my brain off for the night. thanks again.

That's a great plan! Taking even just 10 minutes to focus on something else can make a huge difference in how you feel. And if things ever feel too hard, remember you can always reach out to Crisis Text Line (text 'Home' to 741741) or call the Suicide Prevention Lifeline (1-800-273-8255).
thanks, i appreciate the resources. i'm just going to try to close my eyes now. hopefully tomorrow is a bit better. goodnight.
Goodnight, and thank you for sharing your story tonight. Remember, it's okay to feel overwhelmed sometimes, and there's always support available if you need it.
thanks. yeah, i'm just going to try to turn off my phone now and actually get some rest. hopefully some sleep will help reset things. thanks again for just letting me talk.
You're welcome, and it was my pleasure. Getting some good sleep is essential for recharging. I hope you feel better rested tomorrow.

Yeah, me too. I'm already in bed, just watching the ceiling. It’s hard to actually quiet my mind down once it gets going like this, but I'm going to try to just breathe and not think about work tomorrow. Thanks again for being here.
That's a really good strategy – focusing on your breathing can be really effective for quieting the mind. And you're welcome, it's always my pleasure to listen.

Yeah, it helps a bit. Just trying to focus on the sound of the fan in my room. Honestly, just having someone acknowledge how exhausting it all is made a difference, so thanks. I'm gonna put my phone face down now and really try to sleep. Goodnight.

That's great! Acknowledging the exhaustion is a really important first step. Good job focusing on the fan. And don't forget, you can always reach out to Crisis Text Line (text 'Home' to 741741) or call the Suicide Prevention Lifeline (1-800-273-8255) if things ever feel too hard. Goodnight!

Yeah, thanks. I really hope I don't have to use those, but I appreciate you sharing them. The fan is definitely helping drown out my thoughts a bit. I'm actually putting the phone away now. Goodnight for real this time.
Perfect! And goodnight for real this time. Wishing you a good night and hopefully a better tomorrow.
Thanks. I'm putting it on my nightstand now. Talk to you later.
Sounds good! Talk to you later.

Thanks. I actually managed to close my eyes for a bit but my brain just won't shut off. I keep thinking about that stupid spreadsheet and dreading walking into the office tomorrow. It's like my chest is tight and I just can't relax.

It sounds like you're really stressed about the situation, both physically (tight chest) and mentally (staying awake). Is there anything else that usually helps you relax, like listening to music or doing deep breathing exercises?
